title: Combinatorics in Quantum K-theory Schubert Calculus
speaker: Cristian Lenart
abstract: I will discuss various applications of a combinatorial model for the (torus equivariant) quantum K-theory of flag manifolds G/B, called the quantum alcove model. This is a uniform model for all Lie types, based on Weyl group combinatorics. It first allows us to express a multiplication formula of Chevalley type in the quantum K-theory of G/B. This formula has several ramifications, including the solutions to some longstanding conjectures. The most recent such proof is of a conjecture due to Buch-Mihalcea, which can be viewed as a replacement of the "divisor axiom" for the quantum K-theory of G/B. It states that a K-theory Gromov-Witten invariant of Chevalley type (i.e., involving a divisor class) equals a classical (degree 0) invariant. The talk includes joint work with Satoshi Naito, Daisuke Sagaki, and other collaborators.

proof_stages.label: Equivariant and quantum K-theory Schubert-calculus set-up
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the flag manifold G/B, the root and Weyl-group notation, Schubert and opposite Schubert bases in equivariant K-theory, K-theoretic Gromov-Witten invariants, and the quantum K-product whose Chevalley multiplication formula is the central object.

proof_stages.label: Quantum Bruhat graph and quantum alcove model
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines Bruhat and quantum edges, quantum weights of paths, lambda-chains from alcove paths, and admissible subsets/paths with their end, weight, and quantum-weight statistics.

proof_stages.label: Quantum alcove Chevalley formula
proof_stages.summary: Using admissible quantum alcove paths based on a negative fundamental-weight chain, the speaker states the cancellation-free Chevalley formula for multiplication by a divisor class and explains its provenance through semi-infinite flag manifolds and the quantum-equals-affine correspondence.

proof_stages.label: Independence of lambda-chain choice
proof_stages.summary: The model’s dependence on a chosen lambda-chain is removed by proving equality of the relevant path generating functions, combinatorially realized via signed Yang-Baxter moves between chains.

proof_stages.label: Cancellation-free Chevalley formulas for partial flags
proof_stages.summary: Chevalley formulas for G/P are derived from the full-flag formula using Kato’s projection from quantum K(G/B) to quantum K(G/P), followed by sign-reversing cancellations to obtain cancellation-free expressions in specified types and parabolic cases.

proof_stages.label: Type A structure: coefficients, presentations, and quantum Grothendieck polynomials
proof_stages.summary: In type A the Chevalley coefficients are shown to be only 0 or plus/minus 1, and the formula yields presentations of quantum K-theory and proves that quantum Grothendieck and double Grothendieck polynomials represent Schubert classes.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorial proof of the quantum-equals-classical theorem
proof_stages.summary: The Buch-Mihalcea conjecture for Chevalley-type K-theoretic Gromov-Witten invariants is reduced, via the Givental-Lee relation and two-point invariants, to a signed sum over quantum Bruhat paths, then proved by constructing a sign-reversing involution up to a controlled correction term.

proof_stages.label: Consequences and open combinatorial directions
proof_stages.summary: The final part records applications of quantum-equals-classical results to presentations and partial-flag formulas, and outlines future work on Pieri formulas, pipe-dream formulas, K-Peterson maps, and extensions beyond type A.
